2606.07036.title: STREAM: Stochastic Riemannian Flow Matching with Anisotropic Decoder for Digital Histopathology Image Generation
2606.07036.paper_summary: Synthetic histopathology image generation addresses critical challenges in computational pathology, including patient privacy and the growing need for large-scale training data for foundation models. Latent diffusion models have dominated the image generation domain, with recent works emphasizing that the choice of latent space is critical to the quality of generated images. Existing state-of-the-art generative models in histopathology use pretrained Vision Foundation Models (VFMs) as conditioning signals, and we observe that this leads to "conditioning collapse," where the conditioning signal dominates the latent space and lowers the quality and diversity of generated samples. Therefore, we instead use pretrained histopathology VFMs as the latent space itself, leveraging their patch-token features that encode rich semantic information. We empirically show that these features are $\ell_2$-normalized and lie on the unit hypersphere $\mathcal{S}^{d-1}$ with strong angular dominance and intrinsic curvature, making them naturally suited for a Riemannian formulation. We therefore present STREAM, the first framework to apply Riemannian flow matching in the pathology domain. STREAM consists of two stages: 1) a bridge-type stochastic perturbation that establishes per-token rectifiability on $\mathcal{S}^{d-1}$ for training a Diffusion Transformer (DiT) in latent space, and 2) a novel anisotropic decoder that allocates robustness to low-energy directions of the velocity-field Jacobian while preserving fidelity along its high-energy directions. Together, STREAM achieves state-of-the-art reconstruction and generation performance on breast and colorectal cancer datasets. The code will be publicly released upon acceptance.
2606.07036.paper_summary_zh: 合成組織病理學影像生成解決了計算病理學中的關鍵挑戰，包括患者隱私和對大規模訓練數據的日益需求，以支持基礎模型。潛在擴散模型在影像生成領域中占據主導地位，最近的研究強調潛在空間的選擇對生成影像的質量至關重要。現有的組織病理學最先進生成模型使用預訓練的視覺基礎模型（VFM）作為條件信號，我們觀察到這導致了“條件崩潰”，即條件信號主導了潛在空間，降低了生成樣本的質量和多樣性。因此，我們改為使用預訓練的組織病理學VFM作為潛在空間本身，利用其編碼豐富語義信息的補丁標記特徵。我們實證表明這些特徵是$\ell_2$-正規化的，並且位於單位超球面$\mathcal{S}^{d-1}$上，具有強大的角度主導性和內在曲率，使其自然適合於黎曼形式。我們因此提出了STREAM，第一個在病理學領域應用黎曼流匹配的框架。STREAM由兩個階段組成：1）一種橋接型隨機擾動，在$\mathcal{S}^{d-1}$上建立每個標記的可整流性，以訓練潛在空間中的擴散Transformer（DiT），以及2）一種新穎的各向異性解碼器，為速度場雅可比的低能方向分配穩健性，同時保持其高能方向的保真度。綜合來看，STREAM在乳腺癌和結直腸癌數據集上達到了最先進的重建和生成性能。代碼將在接受後公開發布。
2606.07036.author: Won June Cho et.al.
2606.07036.authors: Won June Cho, Daeky Jeong, Hyeongyeol Lim, Hongjun Yoon
